rulings.Case Title: NORMAN BARTSCH HERTERICH DR VS. ARNDT PELTNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 21, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF NORMAN HERTERICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is unopposed and granted. Code complaint responses to Form and Special Interrogatories shall be served by defendant, Alice Traeg, within fourteen days of service of the Order herein with Notice of Entry. Sanctions are awarded in the sum of $760.00 to be paid within twenty days of service if said Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LISA DIGIOVANNI VS. IVRIA BEN-OR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 21, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT IVRIA BEN-OR'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Compel responses for discovery requests set for hearing on shortened time is denied based on improper notice. The moving party failed to comply with the Order of Judge Goldsmith specially setting of the motion for this date. Said Order required service of both the moving papers and Ex-parte Application and Order. The ex-parte application and Order were not served. As a further basis for denial of the motion based on improper notice, the Notice of Motion served sets the hearing for August 15, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Continue Stay Of Enforcement During Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 21, 2015, Line 14, Motion To Continue Stay Of Enforcement During Appeal. Granted in part. The stay order will remain in effect provided that Defendant posts a bond that complies with the following terms within 20 days of entry of this Order. Defendant is appealing a judgment in the amount of $31,311,759.93. On July 9, 2015, the U.S. District Court, Northern District of California, issued an Order placing $34,178,328.00 owed to Mr. Alioto in attorney's fees in an unrelated action in the constructive custody of this Court. Defendant must post an additional bond that complies with one of the following three options: (1) deposit an additional $12,639,311.90 with the Court; (2) post a surety bond in the amount of $12,639,311.90, or (3) post a personal bond in the amount of $28,445,191.86. (See CCP §§ 917.1(b), 917.2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FEI SU VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Synergy Project Management, Incs Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 21, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FEI SU'S Motion To Compel Defendant Synergy Project Management, Incs Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion is granted. The court overrules the objections and defendant must produce any documents falling within categories 3, 4, 7, 8, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, in the deposition notice of SPM, Inc. by August 14, 2015. The parties shall schedule the depositions of the various persons most knowledgeable on the specified issues in logical sequence. Defendant Javad Mirsaidi must testify if he is the person most knowledgeable on any issues specified in the notice. The court awards sanctions in the amount of $1,020.00. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL TINNISH VS. IMAD ABUMERI M.D ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 21, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY HOSPITALS OF BAKERSFIELD, BAKERSFIELD MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. The motion to transfer venue is denied. A wider venue choice is permitted in actions against corporations or other entities than in actions against individual defendants. Actions against a corporation are triable: (1) where it has its principal place of business (its "residence" for venue purposes); or (2) where the contract was made or to be performed (whether specified in writing or not); or (3) where the obligation or liability arose or the breach occurred. [See CCP § 395.5; Anaheim Extrusion Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Classic Molding Co.) (1985) 170 CA3d 1201, 120.] Here, Defendant Dignity Health's principal place of business is in San Francisco County, therefore San Francisco is the proper venue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
